# CLAUDE.md — agent-ready

## Critical Commands

Use **only** these commands. Never invent alternatives or substitute similar-looking tools.

```bash
# Install (end-user, with LLM support)
pip install "git+https://github.com/vb-nattamai/agent-ready.git[ai]"

# Build (development, editable install with dev extras)
pip install -e '.[dev]'

# Test (must pass with ≥50% coverage — do not relax the threshold)
pytest -q --cov=src/agent_ready --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=50

# Run (transform a target repository)
agent-ready --target /path/to/repo

# Lint check
ruff check src/agent_ready tests

# Format check (do not auto-apply in CI)
ruff format --check src/agent_ready tests

# Dry-run (preview generated files without writing)
agent-ready --target /path/to/repo --dry-run

# Evaluate existing context files without regenerating
agent-ready --target /path/to/repo --eval-only
```

---

## Always Do

1. **Set `PYTHONPATH` when running tests locally** if imports fail: `PYTHONPATH=src pytest -q ...`. The package lives under `src/agent_ready/` and uses the `src` layout declared in `pyproject.toml`.

2. **Keep `src/agent_ready/__init__.py` and the `VERSION` file in sync.** The `__version__` string in `__init__.py` is the authoritative source read by setuptools dynamic versioning; `VERSION` must match. The release workflow manages this automatically — if you touch either file manually, update both.

3. **Add tests under `tests/` for any new code in `src/agent_ready/`.** The CI gate is `--cov-fail-under=50`; new untested code will fail the pipeline. Mirror the module path: code in `src/agent_ready/evaluator.py` → tests in `tests/test_evaluator.py`.

4. **Mock LLM calls at the retry-wrapper level in tests, not at the raw `litellm.completion` level.** Because `analyser.py`, `generator.py`, and `evaluator.py` all wrap LLM calls with exponential-backoff retry logic for 429/529/overloaded errors, mocking only `litellm.completion` may not intercept at the right point. Verify your mock actually short-circuits the retry loop.

5. **Use argument lists (never `shell=True`) in any `subprocess` call.** This is a hard security boundary enforced in `reviewer.py` — all PR diff content is treated as untrusted input. Follow the same pattern everywhere.

6. **Respect the `SKIP_AGENT_FILES` set in `src/agent_ready/analyser.py`** when adding new output filenames. If AgentReady generates a new scaffolding file, add its filename to `SKIP_AGENT_FILES` to prevent circular re-ingestion on the next run.

7. **Use `snake_case` for all new Python identifiers** — modules, functions, variables, and parameters. This is the enforced naming convention across `src/agent_ready/`.

8. **Run `ruff check` and `ruff format --check` before opening a PR.** The CI pipeline enforces both. Note: `src/agent_ready/templates/` is excluded from ruff by design — do not attempt to lint files there.

---

## Never Do

1. **Never modify `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `.github/workflows/release.yml`, or `.github/workflows/codeql.yml`** without explicit human approval. These are listed in `restricted_write_paths` and govern CI/CD integrity and security scanning.

2. **Never commit API keys, tokens, or secrets to any file.** Environment variables (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_API_KEY`, `GROQ_API_KEY`, `MISTRAL_API_KEY`, `TOGETHER_API_KEY`, `INSTALL_TOKEN`, `GH_TOKEN`) must remain outside version control at all times.

3. **Never edit the `VERSION` file directly.** It is managed exclusively by `.github/workflows/release.yml`. Manual edits will desync it from `src/agent_ready/__init__.py` and break the dynamic version resolution.

4. **Never remove or modify `LICENSE`.** It is a restricted write path.

5. **Never add Jinja2 syntax or any template-engine logic to files under `src/agent_ready/templates/`.** Placeholders like `{{TOOL_NAME}}` are plain Python `str.replace()` targets — not Jinja2 tokens. Adding `{% %}` or `{{ }}` Jinja2 constructs will break the substitution logic silently.

6. **Never push directly to `main`.** All changes must go through pull requests. The reusable transformer workflow (`.github/workflows/reusable-transformer.yml`) and drift detector both operate against PR branches.

7. **Never use `shell=True` in `subprocess` calls anywhere in `src/agent_ready/`.** See `reviewer.py` for the correct pattern: pass a list of arguments, never a shell string.

8. **Never modify `pyproject.toml`'s version configuration.** The version is read dynamically from `src/agent_ready/__init__.py` via setuptools' `dynamic` key — changing the `[tool.setuptools.dynamic]` block or hardcoding a version will break packaging.

---

## Architecture Notes

### 1. Five-Phase Pipeline (Collect → Analyse → Generate → Score → Evaluate)
The core pipeline in `src/agent_ready/cli.py` runs five sequential phases. **Phase 1 (Collect)** is purely mechanical file I/O in `analyser.py` — no LLM. **Phase 2 (Analyse)** sends collected files to the strongest available model for structured JSON understanding. **Phase 3 (Generate)** uses an LLM to write up to 12 scaffolding files from that JSON. **Phase 4 (Score)** computes a 100-point readiness score from the analysis. **Phase 5 (Evaluate)** in `evaluator.py` loads a versioned golden question set (13 base + language overlay = 13–19 questions), extracts ground truth from raw source code (non-circular), runs a weak-model baseline, and measures response quality with the full context bundle. Each phase is isolated — if you add a feature, identify which phase owns it before editing.

### 2. Provider-Agnostic via LiteLLM with Tiered Model Selection
`src/agent_ready/cli.py` selects LLM providers through named **provider presets**, each mapping three pipeline phases (analysis, generation, evaluation) to distinct model strings. The analysis phase always uses the strongest model; evaluation uses the fastest. Passing `--model` on the CLI **overrides all three phases** with a single model — useful for testing but loses the tier optimization. LiteLLM (installed via the `[ai]` extra) handles all provider routing; the core package has **zero hard LLM dependencies** and will `ImportError` if `litellm` is absent.

### 3. Circular Analysis Prevention via `SKIP_AGENT_FILES`
`src/agent_ready/analyser.py` maintains a `SKIP_AGENT_FILES` set containing the names of all files AgentReady itself generates (`AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `agent-context.json`, `system_prompt.md`, `memory/schema.md`, `mcp.json`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, etc.). The Phase 1 collector skips these files when reading a target repo, preventing the LLM from reasoning about stale scaffolding instead of actual source code. Any new output file added to the generator **must** be added to this set.

### 4. PR Reviewer is a Separate, Trust-Isolated Module
`src/agent_ready/reviewer.py` is a distinct execution path from the main transformation pipeline. It reads `agent-context.json` from a target repo, fetches PR diffs via the GitHub API, and sends them to an LLM for structured review comments. All PR diff content is treated as **untrusted** — it is forwarded to LLM APIs but never parsed as code or executed locally. This module is invoked via `agent-ready --review` and requires `GH_TOKEN` and `PR_NUMBER` environment variables.

---

## Domain Context

| Term | Meaning in this codebase |
|---|---|
| **scaffolding files** | The up-to-12 files AgentReady writes: `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `system_prompt.md`, `agent-context.json`, `mcp.json`, `memory/schema.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `tools/refresh_context.py`, `.github/dependabot.yml`, `.github/CODEOWNERS`, `openapi.yaml` (REST APIs), `.agent-ready/custom_questions.json`. Together they give AI agents verified, grounded knowledge of a repository. |
| **agent-context.json** | Machine-readable JSON with both a static section (human-editable, persisted across runs) and a dynamic section (overwritten each run by the LLM). Do not conflate the two sections when reading or writing. |
| **readiness score** | A 100-point integer computed in Phase 4 from the analysis JSON. It measures how much structured knowledge the generated files provide to an AI agent. This score is surfaced in PR descriptions by the transformer workflow. |
| **provider preset** | A named constant in `cli.py` mapping `{"analysis": "...", "generation": "...", "evaluation": "..."}` model strings. Presets exist for Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Groq, Mistral, Together, and Ollama. |
| **context drift** | Staleness of generated scaffolding files relative to actual code. Detected weekly by `.github/workflows/context-drift-detector.yml`, which re-runs Phase 2 (Analyse) and diffs the output against committed files. |
| **restricted_write_paths** | Paths no agent (including this one) may modify: `LICENSE`, `VERSION`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `.github/workflows/release.yml`, `.github/workflows/codeql.yml`. |
| **workflow templates** | YAML workflow files in `src/agent_ready/templates/` installed into target repos. These are NOT language-specific MCP tool scaffolds — no Python/Java/Go tool stubs exist. The MCP server is `src/agent_ready/mcp_server.py`. |

---

## Known Pitfalls — Read Before Writing Any Code

> **This section is critical. Read every item before modifying any file.**

1. **Zero hard dependencies** — core package uses only stdlib; LLM features require installing with [ai] extra (`pip install agent-ready[ai]`) or litellm will ImportError.

2. **Version is managed dynamically** via setuptools reading `__version__` from `src/agent_ready/__init__.py` AND a separate `VERSION` file — both must stay in sync (release workflow handles this).

3. **The analyser skips AgentReady-generated files** (`AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `agent-context.json`, etc.) via `SKIP_AGENT_FILES` set — feeding them back would create circular analysis.

4. **Template files use double-brace placeholders** like `{{TOOL_NAME}}` that look like Jinja2 but are NOT — they are simple string replacements; do not add Jinja2 logic.

5. **LLM API calls include retry logic with exponential backoff** for 429/529/overloaded errors — tests mocking LLM calls must account for the retry wrapper.

6. **The ruff exclude configuration explicitly skips `src/agent_ready/templates`** — template files intentionally contain placeholder syntax that would fail linting.

7. **PR reviewer treats all PR diff content as untrusted** — it is sent to LLM APIs but never executed locally; subprocess calls use argument lists (no `shell=True`).

8. **The CI workflow enforces minimum 50% code coverage** (`--cov-fail-under=50`) — new code must maintain this threshold.

9. **Provider presets map three different models per provider** (analysis/generation/evaluation) — using `--model` overrides ALL three phases with the same model.

10. **GitHub Actions workflows use `secrets: inherit` pattern** — `INSTALL_TOKEN` and provider API keys must be set in the target repo's secrets, not just agent-ready's secrets.

---
